q.en: What made Samantha so angry at the beginning of the story?
q.zh: 故事开头，萨曼莎为什么那么生气？

options.en: She lost a basketball game in the garage
options.zh: 因为她在车库里打篮球比赛输了

options.en: Her family moved from Pennsylvania to North Carolina for Dad's new job
options.zh: 因为爸爸换了新工作，全家从宾夕法尼亚搬到了北卡罗来纳

options.en: Her grandma taped quotes all over the bathroom mirrors according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 因为奶奶把名言贴满了浴室镜子（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Her friends moved away without telling her
options.zh: 因为她的朋友们不声不响地搬走了

explain.en: Samantha was furious because her dad's new job forced the family to move far from friends and relatives in Pennsylvania to North Carolina.
explain.zh: 萨曼莎很生气，是因为爸爸的新工作让全家不得不从宾夕法尼亚搬到很远北卡罗来纳，离开了朋友和亲人。

q.en: How does Samantha try to make up with Grandma after her outburst?
q.zh: 萨曼莎发完脾气后，是怎么向奶奶道歉、和好的？

options.en: She buys Grandma caramel-covered flan
options.zh: 她给奶奶买了焦糖布丁

options.en: She makes a collage of grandmother quotes and gives it as an apology card
options.zh: 她做了一张贴满关于奶奶名言的拼贴画，当作道歉卡片送奶奶

options.en: She teaches Grandma how to play chess
options.zh: 她教奶奶下国际象棋

options.en: She writes a mystery story starring Grandma according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 她写了一篇以奶奶为主角侦探的悬疑故事（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Samantha searches for quotes about grandmothers online and makes a decorated collage card to celebrate Grandma and apologize for her behavior.
explain.zh: 萨曼莎上网找了关于奶奶的名言，做成一张装饰精美的拼贴卡片，既赞美奶奶，也为自己的无礼道歉。

q.en: The repeated sound "Boing... being..." appears when Samantha is
q.zh: 文中反复出现的“Boing... being...”声音，是在萨曼莎做什么时出现的？

options.en: reading her poetry aloud
options.zh: 大声朗读自己写的诗

options.en: singing karaoke with Grandma according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 和奶奶一起唱卡拉OK（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: playing the piano sadly
options.zh: 伤心地弹钢琴

options.en: whacking a basketball against the garage door to release her feelings
options.zh: 把篮球砸向车库门，借以发泄情绪

explain.en: Samantha whacks the basketball against the garage door, and the bouncing sound "Boing... being..." echoes her restless, irritated mood.
explain.zh: 萨曼莎把篮球一下下砸向车库门，球弹起时“Boing... being...”的声音，正映出她烦躁不安的心情。

q.en: The simile "my heart pounded like hail on a roof" shows that Samantha felt
q.zh: “我的心像冰雹砸在屋顶上一样怦怦直跳”这个比喻，说明萨曼莎当时感到

options.en: angry and upset
options.zh: 愤怒又难过

options.en: excited about the move
options.zh: 对搬家感到兴奋

options.en: tired from unpacking
options.zh: 因为拆行李而疲惫

options.en: frightened of the movers
options.zh: 害怕那些搬家工人

explain.en: The comparison to hail pounding a roof emphasizes how loudly and violently her anger was beating inside her during the argument.
explain.zh: 把心跳比作冰雹砸屋顶，凸显出争吵时她心里的怒火又响又猛地翻腾。

q.en: Grandma quotes Aristotle: "In all things of nature there is something of the marvelous." She says this to
q.zh: 奶奶引用亚里士多德的话：“万物之中，皆有奇妙之处。”她这么说是为了

options.en: point out the beauty of their green, lush new surroundings
options.zh: 指出新家四周草木青翠、生机盎然的美

options.en: complain about the hot, steamy weather
options.zh: 抱怨天气又热又闷

options.en: teach Samantha how to speak Spanish
options.zh: 教萨曼莎说西班牙语

options.en: explain why she writes mystery novels according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 解释自己为什么写悬疑小说（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Grandma uses Aristotle's quote to remind Samantha to notice the marvelous, green, lush nature around their new North Carolina home.
explain.zh: 奶奶引用亚里士多德，是想提醒萨曼莎去发现新家所在北卡罗来纳那片青翠繁茂的自然之美。

q.en: Which of these is identified in the story as an Italian proverb?
q.zh: 故事里被明确说成是“意大利谚语”的是下面哪一句？

options.en: "Courage is being scared to death—but saddling up anyway" (John Wayne)
options.zh: “勇气就是怕得要死，却仍然上马出发”（约翰·韦恩）

options.en: "Do not anticipate trouble..." (Benjamin Franklin)
options.zh: “不要预想麻烦……”（本杰明·富兰克林）

options.en: "If nothing is going well, call your grandmother"
options.zh: “如果一切都不顺，就打电话给奶奶”

options.en: "In all things of nature there is something of the marvelous" (Aristotle)
options.zh: “万物之中，皆有奇妙之处”（亚里士多德）

explain.en: In Samantha's apology collage, the line "If nothing is going well, call your grandmother" is labeled as an Italian proverb.
explain.zh: 在萨曼莎的道歉拼贴画里，“如果一切都不顺，就打电话给奶奶”被注明是一句意大利谚语。

q.en: When Samantha yells at Grandma, the author describes Grandma's eyes as "concerned and stern" instead of stating her feelings. This choice
q.zh: 萨曼莎冲奶奶大吼时，作者没有直接写奶奶的想法，而是描写她“既关切又严厉”的眼神。这样写是为了

options.en: lets readers infer Grandma's disapproval without being told directly
options.zh: 让读者自己去体会奶奶的不满，而不是直接说出来

options.en: shows that Grandma was laughing
options.zh: 表明奶奶其实在笑

options.en: proves Grandma agreed with Samantha
options.zh: 证明奶奶同意萨曼莎的话

options.en: tells the reader the exact words Grandma said according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 把奶奶说的话原原本本告诉读者（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: By showing Grandma's eyes rather than stating her thoughts, the author lets readers feel her silent disapproval through description, not explanation.
explain.zh: 作者只描写奶奶的眼神，不直接说明她的想法，是让读者从描写中自己感受到她无声的责备。

q.en: The main theme of the story is best described as
q.zh: 这个故事的主题，最准确的概括是

options.en: why moving to a new state is always a mistake according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 为什么搬到外州永远是个错误（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: how to win a game of chess
options.zh: 怎样下赢一盘国际象棋

options.en: learning to cope with change through family love and courage
options.zh: 在亲情与勇气的帮助下，学会面对生活的变化

options.en: how to write a best-selling mystery novel
options.zh: 怎样写出一本畅销的悬疑小说

explain.en: The story follows Samantha from angry resistance to brave acceptance, showing that family love and courage help us cope with big changes.
explain.zh: 故事写萨曼莎从愤怒抗拒到勇敢接受，说明亲情与勇气能帮我们度过生活中的巨大改变。

q.en: How did Grandma cope with her anger when Grandpa died?
q.zh: 爷爷去世时，奶奶是怎么排解自己的愤怒的？

options.en: She taped quotes to the mirror according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 她把名言贴到镜子上（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: She moved to a new state
options.zh: 她搬到了另一个州

options.en: She played basketball
options.zh: 她去打篮球

options.en: She furiously wrote in her journal
options.zh: 她把满腔怒火写进了日记里

explain.en: Grandma tells Samantha that when Grandpa died she was full of rage, and she coped by furiously writing in her journal.
explain.zh: 奶奶告诉萨曼莎，爷爷去世时她满心怒火，是靠拼命写日记才熬过来的。

q.en: By the end of the story, Samantha has changed from angry and resentful to
q.zh: 到故事结尾，萨曼莎从愤怒、怨恨，变成了

options.en: a famous published mystery writer according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 一位出名的悬疑小说作家（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: brave and willing to "saddle up"
options.zh: 勇敢、愿意“跨上马出发”

options.en: a person who never gets upset
options.zh: 一个再也不会难过的人

options.en: someone who hates writing poetry
options.zh: 一个讨厌写诗的人

explain.en: After apologizing and hearing Grandma's John Wayne quote, Samantha finds the strength to be brave and "saddle up" against her fears.
explain.zh: 道歉之后，听到奶奶引用的约翰·韦恩名言，萨曼莎鼓起勇气，愿意“跨上马出发”去面对害怕的事。

prompt.en: Think about a time you felt angry about a change in your life. Write about how you handled it and what helped you feel better.
prompt.zh: 想一想你曾因为生活中的某次改变而生气的时候。写一写你是怎么面对的，又是什么让你感觉好起来。

en: Notice how Samantha's feelings change from the start to the end of the story.
Look for the quotes Grandma shares and how they guide Samantha's actions.
zh: 留意萨曼莎在故事开头和结尾时心情的变化。
找一找奶奶分享的那些名言，看看它们怎样影响了萨曼莎的做法。
